_readme: 33 chronicle-relevant words curated from lexicon/patrinaic.json. All 'con'/'gloss'/'pron' values are copied EXACTLY from the dictionary (gloss = the loc-field term chosen as primary for this concept; the full loc field, comma-separated, is given as 'loc_full' for context). Concepts the dictionary has no headword for are listed in _missing with a one-line reason.

grain.loc_full: Egg, Ovum, Nit, Roe, Spawn, Seed, Grain, Beginning, Start, Origin, Genesis, Germ, Offspring, Progeny, Descendant
grain.note: polysemous root spanning grain/seed/egg/origin — 'grain' is one sense among many, not a dedicated word

death.loc_full: Death, Good Death, Demise, End, Passing
death.note: carries 'Good Death' as an attested sense — an honorable/appropriate death is lexically distinguished, useful bronze-age chronicle color

battle.loc_full: Battle, Combat, Conflict, Engagement, Action, Campaign, Offensive, Operation, Expedition, Electioneering

peace.note: matches PATRINAIC_ROOTS' toponymic root ['Laego','peace'] in index.html, and — outside this study's settlement scope but worth flagging — parses cleanly as the base of the Kembar god-name Laegos: Laego (vowel-final) + -(g)os connector = 'Laegos' exactly per the coded connector rule

oath.loc_full: Swear, Promise, Take an Oath, Vow

king.loc_full: King, Queen, Ruler, Monarch, Sovereign, Head of State, Chief, Tyrant, Potentate
king.note: single genderless term covers both king and queen

queen.loc_full: King, Queen, Ruler, Monarch, Sovereign, Head of State, Chief, Tyrant, Potentate
queen.note: same word as 'king' — see note there

crown.loc_full: Crown, Ordain, Swear In, Enthrone, Inaugurate
crown.note: verb — 'to crown', not the physical object

caravan.loc_full: Train, Procession, Line, File, Column, Caravan, Convoy

market.loc_full: Public Place, Public Square, Open Area, Marketplace, Plaza, Forum
market.note: the place/square, not the abstract institution of trade

gold.note: distinct root from the verb Pharze 'to gild' that Pharzeban derives from — see out-A/canon-etym.json

god.loc_full: Minor God, Minor Deity, Numen, Spirit
god.note: the dictionary's own definition specifies this is a LOCAL/minor deity 'as opposed to the Kembar' — there is no separate attested word in this dataset for the nine great gods themselves, so don't use Ios for a Kembar

rite.loc_full: Spell, Cantrip, Enchantment, Incantation, Charm, Curse, Ritual, Rite
rite.note: shared with magical/spell vocabulary — rite and cantrip are the same word

omen.loc_full: Charred, Singed, Scorched, Burned, Blackened, Ill-Omened
omen.note: primary sense is 'charred/burnt'; 'ill-omened' is an attested figurative extension, not a dedicated omen-noun — no positive/neutral 'omen' word found

star.loc_full: Star, Constellation, Meteor, Shooting Star, Pupil

moon.note: same word covers Nīmlad's moon(s) and Tamar the gas giant itself, per the dictionary's own definition — the campaign's central astronomical fact is baked into the lexicon

eclipse.note: the same word means 'nap' — a striking fit for the campaign's daily 'dark hour' eclipse caused by Tamar, as if the world naps through it

storm.loc_full: Storm, Thunderstorm, Tempest, Gale, Squall, Hurricane

winter.loc_full: Winter, Flood Season, Late Autumn, Late Fall

fire.loc_full: Fire, Soul, Spirit, Life Force, Firewood
fire.note: fire and 'soul/life force' are the same word — useful for funerary/pyre chronicle prose

serpent.loc_full: Snake, Serpent, Viper, Asp, Adder, Cobra

ruin.note: fits Lepon's own canon status as a ruin since the Sack of 745 A.B.

_missing: hunger — no dedicated headword found (only 'Mābar' Devour/Gobble/Wolf, an eating-verb, not the state of hunger)
plague — no headword found; closest is 'Gehās' Nausea/Sickness/Illness (id 1870), a general-sickness word, not epidemic-specific

temple (religious building) — 'Māgeth' (id 1965) glosses 'Forehead, Brow, Temple' but is the anatomical temple; 'Lektān' (priest) and 'Bash' (tower, sometimes 'part of a... temple') are adjacent but neither is the building itself
comet — no headword found (distinct from 'Gimīl' star/meteor/shooting star, id 667, which doesn't cover a comet's tail)
wedding — no event-noun found; only the verb 'Gendresh' (id 867) 'Marry' exists
birth — no event-noun found; only proxies 'Dudsined' Pregnant (id 1939) and 'Ben'/'Mīk' Baby Boy/Baby Girl-Newborn (ids 1651/1652)
